citations.content: - Write a weekly newsletter covering all the AI news from the week

all_text_in_order.value:
All About AI Models
Andrew Mead
Vector Lab
About Me
Andrew Mead
- Machine Learning Engineer
- Former Amazon Alexa
- Currently doing freelance AI consulting
- Write a weekly newsletter covering all the AI news from the week
Roadmap
1. Tokens
  a. What are they
  b. How do they affect the model
2. Models
  a. Best models right now
  b. Multimodal
  c. Self hosting?
  d. Finetuning?
Ask questions anytime!
What are tokens?
Models do math with numbers, not words or images
Tokenization converts the words into numbers for us
Token Estimates
Rule of thumb: 3 words are 4 tokens (on average)
750 words is ~1,000 tokens
The Bible is ~1 million tokens
Humans read at 5-10 tokens per second
Understanding Token Counts
| Tokens | Words | Category |
| :--------- | :----------- | :------------- |
| 1,000 | ~750 words | Blog article |
| 4,000 | ~3,000 words | Essay |
| 128,000 | ~96,000 words| Novel |
| 256,000 | ~192,000 words| Long novel |
| 1 Million | ~750,000 words| Textbook |
| 2 Million | ~1,500,000 words| Encyclopedia |
What the model sees ☐
Input tokens
  - System and user prompts
  - Tools
  - Images or audio
- Documents from RAG
Can be cached for large savings
What the model gives
Output tokens
  - Reasoning chains
- Tool calls
  - Final model response
Token Ratios
Majority of your tokens are usually input
- Input tokens are usually 5-10x cheaper than output tokens
Prices are increased for long sequence lengths
- For instance, Gemini costs 2x more when input seq len > 200k
Paid Tier, per 1M tokens in USD
$2.00, prompts <= 200k tokens
$4.00, prompts > 200k tokens
How many tokens can a model take?
Between 128k to 1 million, depending on the model.
| Model | Input Tokens | Output Tokens |
| GPT-5.2 | 400k | 128k |
| Gemini 3 Pro | 1,000k | 64k |
| Claude Sonnet 4.5 | 200k | 64k |
Source: Vector Lab
Can they use this context window?
No, performance degrades as sequence length increases
Fiction.live bench measures QA ability given a passage from a book, with increasing amount of context
What is a reasoning model?
“Think before you answer”
Model generates hidden tokens before answering
More reasoning -> more latency
Not all reasoning models are the same
Gives performance boost for scientific and planning tasks
Some models, like GPT-5, allow you to control the amount of reasoning (high, medium, low, minimal)
Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index
Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v3.0 incorporates 10 evaluations: MMLU-Pro, GPQA Diamond, Humanity's Last Exam, LiveCodeBench, SciCode, AIME 2025, IFBench, AA-LCR, Terminal-Bench Hard, 2-Bench Telecom
There’s a bunch of models out there
OpenAI
GPT 5.2
- Smartest model out there
- A bit cold and uninteresting
- Intelligence varies a lot with reasoning
- Strong agentic capabilities
- Top 2 coding model
Anthropic
Opus 4.6
- Only model that can compete with GPT-5.2
- Good for vibe coding
- Great conversationalist
- Also very good agent
Sonnet 4.6
- Little brother of Opus
"Tier 2" model
Haiku 4.5
- Smallest in the family
Google
Gemini 3 Flash
- Small, cheap fast
- Start here with your products
Gemini Pro cant compete with the top dogs
Both are top multimodal models
Everyone Else
MINIMAX
GLM 5, MiniMax 2.5
- Open source, cheap, Sonnet 4.5 quality coding agent
Kimi K2.5: Interesting to talk to
Qwen: Best open source small models
Grok 4: Meh
Llama: Old, use Qwen instead
Model Cheat Sheet
Top Models: GPT 5.2 , Opus 4.6
Price to performance: Gemini 3 Flash, GLM 5, MiniMax 2.5
Multimodal: Gemini 3 Pro
Qwen3
Small models: Qwen
Best conversationalists: Kimi K2, Opus 4.5
Model Pricing Cheat Sheet
| Model | $ per million (input) | $ per million (output) |
| Claude Sonnet 4.6 | $3 | $15 |
| Claude Opus 4.6 | $5 | $25 |
| GPT 5.2 | $1.75 | $14 |
| Gemini 3 Pro | $2 | $12 |
| GLM 5 | $1.00 | $3.20 |
| Gemini 3 Flash | $0.50 | $3 133 |
| MiniMax M2.5 | $0.30 | $1.20 |
Self hosting models
We have these open source model’s, what if I hosted them myself
- Increased privacy
- Lower costs?
- Fine tune my own model?
Let’s do some math
Instead of using Gemini 3 Flash ($3 per million output tokens), you want to self host GLM 5. To host GLM 5, you need 8 H100 GPUs, which cost $2/hour/gpu.
How many tokens could you get from Gemini 3 Flash for the same price as renting 8 H100s for a month?
Solution
Cost of gpus per month = $2*8*24*30 = $11520
Number of Gemini tokens for breakeven = $11520/($3/mil tokens) = 3.84 billion tokens
Obviously not all your tokens are output (input tokens cost 5x less)
Feel free to retry with a 20:1 input:output token ratio for a more realistic number (it’s even bigger)
Self hosting downsides.
- Increased Devops
- GPUs are not reliable
- No privacy gains vs AWS Bedrock or Azure AI foundry
- Need consistent, high usage to justify costs
- Constantly changing models
- Open source models tend to be ~6 month behind closed source
Amazon Bedrock
Azure AI Foundry
Should I finetune?
We have a bunch of proprietary data, we should finetune a model to use this! Don’t do this
- Takes way more effort than expected
- Especially data engineering
- Frontier models are very good at in context learning (ICL)
LLM -> Curated dataset -> Fine-tuned LLM
What you should do before finetuning
Get the model the fight context!
- Manual or automatic prompt optimization
- RAG (search)
- Tool use/ MCP servers
- DSPY
- MCP (Model Context Protocol)
Questions?
Vector Lab
Free weekly AI newsletter!
vectorlab.dev
